page.content: In recent years, the North American food and beverage sector has increasingly prioritized sustainability, particularly in packaging and raw material sourcing. This shift is driven by consumer demand for more environmentally responsible products and the need to mitigate the impacts of climate change. The accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of key metrics related to packaging sustainability, raw material cost trends, and the adoption of plant-based products within this sector. Notably, the data highlights the current costs associated with plastic packaging, showcasing a significant investment of $200 million, as well as trends in sugar pricing, which stands at $15 million.

lines.text: In recent years, the North American food and beverage sector has increasingly prioritized sustainability, particularly in
packaging and raw material sourcing. This shift is driven by consumer demand for more environmentally responsible products
and the need to mitigate the impacts of climate change. The accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of key
metrics related to packaging sustainability, raw material cost trends, and the adoption of plant-based products within this sector.
Notably, the data highlights the current costs associated with plastic packaging, showcasing a significant investment of $200
million, as well as trends in sugar pricing, which stands at $15 million.

page.content: Additionally, the market share of plant-based products has reached 15.3%, reflecting a growing consumer inclination towards healthier and more sustainable options.
